---
schema: qual/card@1
id: STR-COUNTSYLOW
kind: strategy
title: Prove nonsimplicity by counting Sylow subgroups
classification:
  areas:
  - algebra
  topics:
  - groups
relations: []
review: draft
---

::: strategy
Bound $n_p$ by the two Sylow constraints, $n_p \equiv 1 \pmod p$ and $n_p \divides m$.
If some prime forces $n_p = 1$ the group is not simple; if not, count elements of
prime order and look for an overflow.
:::
